summary: Session 3: `Switch Tier…` action shipped per D4 — new pure byte-preserving rewrite helper src/utils/tierRewrite.ts (rewriteSpecTier: config-block-scoped tier rewrite mirroring parseSessionSetConfig's regexes; preserves quote style / trailing comments / CRLF; absent-key insertion for lightweight, already-target for full; commented lines immune; malformed scalars repaired; non-config tier strings untouched) plus switchToFullWarnings guardrail predicate reusing providerKeyPresent. New command dabblerSessionSets.switchTier (commands/switchTier.ts): not-started re-check, tier QuickPick reusing the exported Getting Started promptTier copy (placeholder names the set + current tier), helper-driven decision, view refresh, outcome-specific messages incl. malformed-scalar 'Repaired'. ActionRegistry flat entry group 504 gated isNotStarted (17 actions); package.json contribution; extension.ts safeRegister; watcherInventory allowlist 190→191/226→227 (import line shift). D4 guardrails inform-only on switch-to-full: missing provider key + missing <workspace>/ai_router/router-config.yaml → points at Dabbler: Install ai-router. Tests: tierRewrite.test.ts 16-case rewrite matrix + 5 guardrail tests; actionRegistry not-started-only gating test. TS 752 pass + 2 pre-existing Set-026 failures; Python 1185 pass / 1 skip; dist committed in-sync. Verification: R1 gpt-5-4 ISSUES_FOUND 2 Major — S061-S3-V1-001 (set.root guardrail base) DISPROVEN empirically (set.root IS the workspace root, fileSystem.ts:420/761; diff context gap); S061-S3-V1-002 half FIXED (early same-tier return removed; helper is single decision authority; typo-repair test) half DISPROVEN (parser lowercases, fileSystem.ts:253; case-variant test). R2 narrow re-verify gpt-5-4: VERIFIED. Routed spend $0.1385. Note for S4 UAT: menu label is Title-Case 'Switch Tier…' vs spec prose 'Switch tier…'.

reason.specifics: Per the routed ai-assignment analysis, Session 4 is release mechanics (UAT checklist authoring, local .vsix build, version bump, tag push on operator authorization) — procedural, file-system-centric work where a CLI-oriented model at medium effort is the cheaper appropriate fit.
